title: Projects
text:
Project 1: Traveller Insight Dashboard (R Shiny)
Goal: Provide interactive exploration of traveller patterns by time, category, and geography.
My work: Data preparation, Shiny architecture, ggplot2 visuals, automated insight text, reproducible structure.
Tools: R, Shiny, ggplot2, dplyr, leaflet/plotly (as used)
Links: GitHub repo • Live demo • Short write-up

title: About
text:
I am a data analytics professional with over 6 years of experience delivering enterprise reporting, data integration, and analytics solutions across multiple industries.
My work focuses on transforming fragmented data and manual reporting into reliable, scalable analytics systems that support operational and strategic decision making. I have experience building analytics products, improving data governance, and delivering solutions that create measurable business impact.
I am particularly interested in how analytics, systems, and governance work together to enable effective data driven organisations.

Business Intelligence & Analytics Professional

I specialise in helping organisations turn fragmented data and inconsistent reporting into trusted, decision-ready analytics used by business and executive stakeholders.

Dubai • Malaysia • Jakarta

6+ years experience • Business Intelligence & Analytics • Australia Awards Scholar

title: Yusuf Kurnia Romadhon
text:
What I do
I help organisations transform fragmented data and manual reporting into reliable analytics systems that support operational and strategic decision making.
My work typically involves:

Leading the definition of business questions and KPIs with stakeholders.
Designing and delivering reporting and analytics solutions aligned to business priorities.
Improving data quality, governance, and documentation to support consistent decision-making.
Presenting insights and recommendations to non-technical and executive audiences.

title: Yusuf Kurnia Romadhon
text:
How I think about analytics
I believe good analytics is not about tools alone. It is about:

Clear problem definition before analysis.
Responsible and ethical use of data.
Reproducibility and documentation.
Insights that lead to action, not just dashboards.
Strong alignment between analytics, business context, and stakeholder needs.

This mindset guides how I design analytics solutions in both professional and applied academic settings.

title: Yusuf Kurnia Romadhon
text:
Selected work
This portfolio highlights a selection of analytics projects, including interactive dashboards, data engineering workflows, ethical data documentation, and applied analytics case studies.
Each project summary focuses on:

The business problem
My role and approach
The practical outcome

Detailed project summaries and links to technical repositories are available on the Projects page.

title: Projects
text: Featured Projects

title: Projects
text:
Project 1: Company Scorecard Dashboard (Power BI)
Goal: Transform manual KPI reporting into an automated, interactive dashboard for executive decision making.
My work: Designed and developed a multi-page Power BI scorecard dashboard, built a hybrid star schema data model, implemented KPI tracking with thresholds, and created custom visuals using Deneb (Vega-Lite).
Tools: Power BI (DAX), Deneb (Vega-Lite), Data Modelling, Quarto, GitHub Pages
Links: Project showcase & documentation • GitHub repository • Power BI (.pbix) available in repository

title: Projects
text:
Project 2: Analytix Product Case Study
Goal: Develop and scale a commercial analytics product providing construction industry insights across projects, sectors, and locations. My work: Built Tableau-based prototype dashboards, supported product development, and contributed to transitioning components into a scalable C++ system.
Impact: Supported customer growth from ~50 to ~150 users and improved product scalability for real-world usage.
Tools: Tableau, SQL, Data Integration
Links: Case study • Product overview
